message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state from concurrent agent work

Updates the beads JSONL issue database to reflect status changes made
by other agents working on the project. This keeps the local issue
tracker in sync with the latest task states.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -384,7 +384,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-6yd","title":"Crash bundle: deterministic evidence + backtrace capture on failure","description":"Critique mapping: #1 (prove; do not hand-wave).

Deliverables:
- When any smoke/fixture fails, capture:
  - backtrace (RUST_BACKTRACE=1)
  - mode + env vars
  - evidence ledger snapshot (TSM repairs/denials)
  - allocator stats snapshot (arena/quarantine)
  - /proc/self/maps excerpt for pointer provenance debugging

Acceptance:
- Bundle generation is deterministic and bounded (no unbounded logs).
- Bundle is sufficient to reproduce the failure locally.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): crash_bundle_test (9 pass) + gate script. Artifact filenames, bounds, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist all validated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.930667114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Crash bundle specification operational. 9 harness tests pass. check_crash_bundle.sh validates evidence snapshots, determinism rules, and reproduction checklist.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","critique","diagnostics"]}
 {"id":"bd-73r","title":"EPIC: Runtime Hot-Path Perf Gates (strict <20ns, hardened <200ns)","description":"Goal: enforce extreme-software-optimization discipline for runtime_math.

Why:
- runtime_math is on the membrane hot path. Any regression here leaks into every libc call we gate.

Constraints:
- Strict mode must stay <20ns/call overhead target.
- Hardened mode must stay <200ns/call overhead target.
- No behavior regressions without explicit isomorphism proof + fixture evidence.

Outputs:
- Microbench + profile baselines for RuntimeMathKernel::decide + observe_validation_result.
- Golden snapshot outputs to detect accidental semantic drift.
- A repeatable baseline/profile/verify loop that future work MUST follow.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.483973301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:28:13.676103762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:28:04.198742106Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-1om","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:02.963092567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:03.182677202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-38w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:03.033841207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-3dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:03.105370048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:02.822099661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:02.892070636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-73r","depends_on_id":"bd-vj3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:07.149122119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-73r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Extreme-Optimization Discipline For runtime_math

This epic encodes the non-negotiable loop: **baseline -> profile -> prove -> implement -> verify -> repeat**.

### What We Are Protecting
runtime_math runs at the ABI boundary of libc call paths. Any accidental overhead shows up everywhere.

Budgets (from AGENTS.md):
- strict overhead target: <20ns/call (fast exits)
- hardened overhead target: <200ns/call (bounded heavy path)

### What Counts As “Hot Path”
- `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` on the strict path
- per-call evidence recording (if enabled)
- pointer-validation stage ordering / cached biases

### Hard Rules
- No transcendental math, matrix solves, or heap allocation on strict fast path.
- Any stochastic element must be deterministic from seed (replayable).
- Any “expensive” kernel must be cadence-driven (epoch update) not per call.

### Measurement Artifacts Required
- Criterion microbench for `decide` / `observe` (ns/op + percentiles)
- Profile recipe (flamegraph/perf) pinned to a reproducible command
- Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate
- Isomorphism proof template for policy changes (behavior invariants)

### Definition Of Done
- We can quantify overhead deltas per kernel addition.
- We can detect regressions in CI before merging.
- We have an explicit narrative for every optimization: what changed, why safe, how measured.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:00Z"},{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-73r","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"EPIC CLOSED by CobaltCompass. All 7 sub-beads complete: (1) bd-d5l microbench (BlueLake), (2) bd-vj3 per-kernel bench suite (BrightMoose, verified CobaltCompass), (3) bd-1om golden snapshots (CobaltForge), (4) bd-pt6 profiling recipe (BlueLake), (5) bd-3dv hard rule audit (CobaltCompass - new enforcement script + invariant test), (6) bd-242 regression thresholds (BlueLake), (7) bd-38w isomorphism proof template (BrightMoose). Infrastructure delivered: scripts/hard_rule_audit.sh, scripts/perf_gate.sh, scripts/snapshot_gate.sh, runtime_math_bench + runtime_math_kernels_bench + perf_baseline.json.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:28:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-747","title":"Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

Acceptance:
- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","env","stubs"]}
-{"id":"bd-7cba","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Minimal codec scope/exclusion ledger + compatibility intent","description":"Background:
- iconv scope must be explicit to avoid overpromising full iconvdata parity before deterministic generation pipeline exists.

Goal:
- Define minimal codec scope, exclusions, and compatibility intent for phase progression.

Deliverables:
1) Codec inclusion/exclusion ledger with rationale.
2) Compatibility goals per included codec family.
3) Support matrix mapping for supported/unsupported codecs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scope is explicit and user-facing claims are accurate.
- No ambiguous codec support status.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for scope manifest parsing/validation.
- Structured logs for scope checks and drift detections.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.290717538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:07.346357347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","iconv","locale","spec","verification"]}

- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist

 {"id":"bd-7dw2","title":"bd-rqn subtask: Decision-law linkage proof suite for all production controllers","description":"Background:
- Production controllers must be proven to actually influence runtime decisions in a measurable and auditable way.

Goal:
- Validate decision-law linkage for each production controller and detect dead/ornamental wiring.

Deliverables:
1) Linkage tests that show controller signals reach decide/observe outcomes.
2) Snapshot/report fields demonstrating active influence.
3) Dead-link detection gate for production set.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every production controller has passing linkage evidence.
- Dead/inactive production controller wiring fails gates.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit and integration tests for linkage paths.
- Structured logs with trace_id, controller_id, signal, influence_result, artifact_refs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.573199672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T23:24:09.950090999Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T23:24:09.811469213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","math","runtime","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":119,"issue_id":"bd-7dw2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-7dw2.

Plan:
1) Define the authoritative production-controller set from  + .
2) Implement a harness-side linkage test that, for each production controller, proves:
   - it is reachable from the hot-path decision law ( / observe hooks), and
   - its cached state is actually read in fusion/severity aggregation (not dead code).
3) Add a deterministic gate script (extended CI) that fails if any production controller lacks linkage proof artifacts.
4) Emit structured JSONL logs: trace_id, controller_id, probe_case, observed_state_delta, influence_result, artifact_refs.

Will coordinate via Agent Mail once MCP load clears (currently health_level=red).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:04Z"},{"id":120,"issue_id":"bd-7dw2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-7dw2 (corrected comment; prior comment had zsh backtick substitution).

Plan:
1) Define the authoritative production-controller set from:
   - tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json
   - tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_classification_matrix.v1.json
2) Implement a harness-side linkage proof suite that, for each production controller, proves:
   - it is reachable from the runtime decision law (RuntimeMathKernel decide/observe hooks), and
   - its cached state is actually read on the hot path (fusion/severity aggregation), not dead/ornamental wiring.
3) Add a deterministic gate script (extended CI) that fails if any production controller lacks linkage proof artifacts.
4) Emit structured JSONL logs with: trace_id, controller_id, probe_case, observed_state_delta, influence_result, artifact_refs.

Coordination: will send Agent Mail updates once MCP load clears (currently health_level=red).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:49Z"},{"id":121,"issue_id":"bd-7dw2","author":"Codex","text":"Closed bd-7dw2.

Delivered:
- Harness subcommand: frankenlibc-harness runtime-math-linkage-proofs
- Gate script: scripts/check_runtime_math_linkage_proofs.sh
- CI wiring: scripts/ci.sh (extended gates)
- Integration tests: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/runtime_math_linkage_proofs_test.rs
- Gate implementation: crates/frankenlibc-harness/src/runtime_math_linkage_proofs.rs

Structured artifacts:
- target/conformance/runtime_math_linkage_proofs.log.jsonl
- target/conformance/runtime_math_linkage_proofs.report.json

Verification:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_linkage_proofs.sh
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_linkage_proofs_test -- --nocapture

Notes:
- The proof gate enforces: every production module in tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json must show decision-law influence (direct decide read or fusion-input linkage). Dead/ornamental wiring fails deterministically with per-module diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:24:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-7ef9","title":"bd-h5x subtask: Callthrough census + decommission sequencing by symbol family","description":"Background:
- Callthrough elimination work needs symbol-family-level truth to avoid hidden dependence on host glibc.

Goal:
- Produce current callthrough census and decommission plan by family/symbol with risk and sequence.

Deliverables:
1) Enumerate all remaining callthrough symbols and their owning modules.
2) Rank by runtime impact and replacement complexity.
3) Define migration order with rollback strategy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is machine-readable and tied to support matrix.
- Decommission plan is dependency-aware and implementation-ready.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for census extraction/parsing helpers.
- Structured logs for census generation and ranking decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberStone","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.598635951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:07.423915839Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:07.423897114Z","close_reason":"Completed support-matrix-tied callthrough census artifact with dependency-aware decommission waves, generator/check scripts, structured log/report emission, and passing gate/verification evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["callthrough","critique","docs","syscall","verification"],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"bd-7ef9","author":"AmberStone","text":"Implemented deterministic callthrough census + dependency-aware decommission sequencing tied to support_matrix.json.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json\n- scripts/generate_callthrough_census.py\n- scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/callthrough_census_test.rs\n- target/conformance/callthrough_census.report.json (generated by gate)\n- target/conformance/callthrough_census.log.jsonl (generated by gate)\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/generate_callthrough_census.py --support-matrix support_matrix.json --output tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json\n- scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-amber cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test callthrough_census_test --no-run\n- /tmp/cargo-target-amber/debug/deps/callthrough_census_test-13fe70897b726eac\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-amber cargo run -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness -- verify --fixture tests/conformance/fixtures --report /tmp/frankenlibc_strict_hardened_verify_bd_7ef9.md\n\nNotes:\n- Artifact captures support-matrix summary drift explicitly (: summary reports 54 vs 49 derived callthrough symbols).\n- Direct 
running 3 tests
test artifact_exists_and_has_required_shape ... ok
test artifact_summary_counts_match_rows ... ok
test gate_script_passes_and_emits_artifacts ... ok

test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.11s hit an intermittent linker bus error in rust-lld while building bin harness in this environment; test binary itself executed cleanly after .","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8sho","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts completion claims require synchronized docs/support matrix/feature parity narratives.

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","drift","hard-parts","verification"],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"},{"id":205,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}
